description: Implement S3 Input/Output Buckets and EventBridge Rule following strict TDD. Write failing tests first in CdkBaseTest.java, then implement the minimal CDK code in CdkBaseStack.java to make them pass.
steps: Step 1 - Write failing tests in src/test/java/com/myorg/CdkBaseTest.java. Add the following test methods using CDK assertions (Template.fromStack, hasResourceProperties, resourceCountIs): (a) testInputBucketExists - verify an S3 bucket resource exists with BucketEncryption configured for SSE-KMS (or aws:kms), VersioningConfiguration Status Enabled, and PublicAccessBlockConfiguration all set to true. (b) testOutputBucketExists - verify a second S3 bucket resource exists with encryption and versioning. (c) testEventBridgeRuleExists - verify an AWS::Events::Rule resource exists with an EventPattern that matches s3 Object Created events from the input bucket, and State ENABLED. (d) testEventBridgeNotificationEnabled - verify the input bucket has NotificationConfiguration or that EventBridge notifications are enabled (the CDK L2 construct sets this up via a custom resource or bucket property). Use Map.of() for property maps. Import java.util.Map and java.util.List as needed.
Step 2 - Run 'mvn test' and confirm all new tests FAIL (red phase). The existing testStackSynthesizes should still pass. Document which tests fail and why.

Step 3 - Implement the S3 Input Bucket in src/main/java/com/myorg/CdkBaseStack.java. Use the L2 Bucket construct (software.amazon.awscdk.services.s3.Bucket) with: bucketName not hardcoded (let CDK generate), encryption set to BucketEncryption.S3_MANAGED or KMS, versioned(true), blockPublicAccess(BlockPublicAccess.BLOCK_ALL), enforceSSL(true), eventBridgeEnabled(true). Give it logical ID 'SleepAudioInputBucket'.

Step 4 - Implement the S3 Output Bucket in CdkBaseStack.java with similar properties: encryption, versioning, blockPublicAccess, enforceSSL. Logical ID 'SleepAudioOutputBucket'. No eventBridgeEnabled needed for output bucket.

Step 5 - Implement the EventBridge Rule in CdkBaseStack.java. Use software.amazon.awscdk.services.events.Rule with an event pattern matching source 'aws.s3', detailType 'Object Created', and detail matching the input bucket. For the target, use a placeholder - either an empty targets list or a simple stub (e.g., a CloudWatch LogGroup target from software.amazon.awscdk.services.events.targets). The rule should be enabled.
Step 6 - Run 'mvn test' and confirm ALL tests pass (green phase). Fix any test assertions that need adjustment based on actual synthesized CloudFormation properties.
Step 7 - Run 'cdk synth' (with unset NODE_OPTIONS) and verify successful synthesis.
Step 8 - Refactor if needed while keeping tests green.
acceptance_criteria: CdkBaseTest.java contains at least 4 test methods: testStackSynthesizes (existing), testInputBucketExists, testOutputBucketExists, testEventBridgeRuleExists
Tests verify encryption, versioning, and public access block on both buckets
Tests verify EventBridge rule event pattern matches S3 Object Created events
Running 'mvn test' passes all tests with 0 failures
CdkBaseStack.java contains SleepAudioInputBucket and SleepAudioOutputBucket L2 constructs
CdkBaseStack.java contains an EventBridge Rule triggered by S3 Object Created events from the input bucket
verification: eval "$(mise activate bash)" && cd /projects/sandbox/cdk-sleep-java-kiro && mvn test
eval "$(mise activate bash)" && cd /projects/sandbox/cdk-sleep-java-kiro && unset NODE_OPTIONS && cdk synth
findings: CDK 2.257.0 Java API uses enforceSsl() not enforceSSL(). The eventBridgeEnabled(true) on a Bucket creates a Custom::S3BucketNotifications resource (via a Lambda-backed custom resource) that can be asserted with resourceCountIs. Tests use Map.of() and List.of() for CDK assertion property maps.
